funding_references.funder_name: National Natural Science Foundation of China
National Natural Science Foundation of China

title: Liu et al. 2023 — Motor Imagery EEG dataset
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 50 acute stroke patients (1-30 days post-stroke) performing motor imagery tasks of left- and right-handed hand-grip movements. Recorded using a wireless 29-channel EEG system (29 EEG + 2 EOG channels) at 500 Hz, the dataset includes raw and preprocessed data, representing the first open resource addressing bilateral motor imagery in acute stroke populations. The data supports brain-computer interface development and rehabilitation applications in clinical stroke recovery.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired from 50 acute stroke patients using a ZhenTec NT1 wireless multichannel system with 29 EEG channels and 2 EOG channels sampled at 500 Hz. Participants performed 40 trials of hand-grip motor imagery (20 left, 20 right) with each 8-second trial consisting of instruction, 4-second video-guided imagery, and rest phases. Data were preprocessed using EEGLAB (MATLAB R2019b) with FIR filtering (0.5-40 Hz bandpass), baseline removal, and artifact rejection. Clinical assessments included NIHSS, MBI, and mRS scores.
